metadata.title: Write the Post-Build Document with Claude: Account for Every Decision

beats.narration_text: Without the post-build document, the lesson evaporates. With it, the next build starts smarter.

The post-build document is the Feynman test applied to your own build: if you can't write specifically what you learned and what you'd change, you borrowed the capability from Codex instead of building it yourself. The five sections are not a retrospective format — they are a teaching artifact. Section 5 is the one that converts experience into transferable discipline.
Generate the 5-section post-build document. Section 5: four specific, reversible decisions — not vague self-criticism.

props.command: claude "I just finished building an interactive bubble-sort simulation for my AP CS class. Help me write the 5-section post-build document. Section 5: What I would do differently — give me 4 specific, reversible decisions, not vague self-criticism. Format as markdown."

beats.narration_text: Read it before trusting it. Five sections: what I built, what I delegated, what I kept, what I learned, what I would do differently — four specific reversible decisions.

props.code:
# Post-Build Document — Bubble Sort Simulator

## 1. What I built
Interactive bubble-sort simulator, AP CS class.

## 2. What I delegated to Codex
Pattern-completion: HTML boilerplate, CSS
variable scaffolding, step-function logic.

## 3. What I kept for myself
DESIGN.md decisions. Pedagogical framing.
Which interactions are allowed vs. never.

## 4. What I learned
The DESIGN.md constraint enforces design
decisions before any code is generated.

## 5. What I would do differently
1. Write PROJECT.md Layer 1 before AGENTS.md.
2. Specify max 4 columns in DESIGN.md tables.
3. Add 'never add CSS outside DESIGN.md
   variables' to AGENTS.md before Code Mode.
4. Run three-pass verification before calling
   Phase 5 complete.

beats.narration_text: Generated document: Section 5 has 4 specific reversible decisions. Each one is actionable: a future teacher knows exactly what to add to AGENTS.md, what value to put in DESIGN.md, and what verification step to run.
Ask Claude to evaluate Section 5 against one criterion: could a future teacher act on each decision without knowing the original builder?

props.command: claude "Evaluate Section 5 of this post-build document: for each of the 4 decisions, would a future teacher be able to act on it without knowing the original builder? Flag any that are underspecified and propose a revision."

beats.narration_text: Evaluation pass: decisions 1, 3, and 4 pass — a future teacher could act on each. Decision 2 flagged: the column count value is not specified. Revised decision 2: specify max 4 columns in DESIGN.md for any table output — confirmed by testing on iPhone SE portrait mode. The Plausibility Audit caught the gap in Claude's own output.

Section 5 is the load-bearing section. Vague self-criticism is not actionable. Specific reversible decisions are actionable: the future teacher knows exactly what to add to AGENTS.md, what value to put in DESIGN.md, and what verification step to run. The specificity is the teaching.
Next: build a plausibility audit checklist that names which supervisory capacity fired at each step.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. At NikBearBrown, nikbearbrown dot com.
